name: Ebingers All Chocolate Blackout Cake
recipeInstructions:
------------------------------FILLING (SEE NOTE------------------------------
1 tbsp + 1 3/4 t Cocoa Powder
2 cups Boiling Water
3/4 cup + 1 T + 1/2 t Sugar
1 oz Bittersweet Chocolate, chopped
2 tbsp Cornstarch dissolved in 1 T of Cold Water
1/4 tsp Salt
1 tsp Vanilla Extract
2 tbsp Butter
----------------------------------FROSTING----------------------------------
12 oz Semisweet Chocolate, chopped
12 tbsp (1 1/2 sticks) Unsalted Butter, softened
1/2 cup Hot Water
1 tbsp Light Corn Syrup
1 tsp Vanilla Extract
1. Preheat oven to 375°F and prepare two 8-inch pans by greasing and flouring.
2. Make the cake: Whisk cocoa with boiling water to form a paste. Combine chocolate and milk in a small saucepan over medium heat, stirring until chocolate melts. Remove from heat, whisk in a small amount of hot chocolate mixture into the cocoa paste to warm it. Whisk the cocoa mixture into the milk mixture. Return the pan to medium heat and stir for 1 minute. Remove and set aside to cool until tepid.
3. In a mixing bowl, cream butter and sugar together. Beat in egg yolks one at a time, then add vanilla. Stir in chocolate mixture. Combine flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt; using a spatula, add the flour mix to the chocolate mixture, folding until just mixed.
4. Whisk egg whites to form soft peaks. Fold into batter; divide batter between the two pans. Bake until a toothpick comes out clean from the center (about 45 minutes). Cool on racks for 15 minutes.
5. While baking cake, make filling: Combine cocoa and boiling water in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir in sugar and chocolate. Add dissolved cornstarch paste and salt to the pan; bring to a boil, stirring continuously. Boil for 1 minute. Remove from heat, whisk in vanilla and butter. Transfer mixture to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate until cool.
6. Make frosting: Melt chocolate in a double boiler over hot (not simmering) water, stirring until smooth. Whisk in butter one tablespoon at a time until melted. Remove from heat; whisk in hot water all at once and whisk until smooth. Whisk in corn syrup and vanilla. Cover and refrigerate for up to 15 minutes prior to using.
7. Assemble the cake: Slice each layer to form four layers, setting one layer aside. Place one layer on a cake plate. Generously spread with about 1/3 of the filling. Add second layer and repeat. Set third layer on top and quickly apply a layer of frosting to the top and sides of the cake. Refrigerate for 10 minutes.
8. Crumble remaining cake layer; apply remaining frosting to the cake, sprinkling liberally with crumbled pieces. Serve within 24 hours. Store in a cool place.
Note: The filling will be runny when warm but hardens as it cools in the refrigerator.
